perturbation.original_text: Except as set forth below, "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any financial, operational, technical and other information relating to the present and future businesses and affairs of the party disclosing the information (the "Disclosing Party"), which information is provided to the other party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the business relationship provided in written, oral, graphic, pictorial or recorded form or stored on computer discs, hard drives, magnetic tape or digital or any other electronic medium (it being understood that oral communications will be confirmed in writing within three (3) working days).
perturbation.changed_text: Except as set forth below, "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any information relating to the present and future businesses and affairs of the party disclosing the information (the "Disclosing Party"), which information is provided to the other party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specifics of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' regarding financial, operational, technical and other data provided, and the detailed way in which it can be provided, Section 5, which restricts the use of 'Confidential Information,' becomes ambiguous. The restriction still applies, but the scope of information considered confidential is now undefined, creating uncertainty. Specifically, Section 5 refers to 'Confidential Information' without the specifics listed in this section, making it vague about what cannot be licensed or used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information,' specifying the types of information and forms it could take. The changed text removes these specifics, leaving the definition vague. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits the use of 'Confidential Information.' Because the changed text no longer defines what 'Confidential Information' encompasses, Section 5's restriction becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, as it's unclear what information is subject to the restriction. This aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction because essential language was removed creating a contradiction with constrains stated in the other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will (i) inform each of its Representatives receiving Confidential Information of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the existence and the terms of this Agreement, (ii) direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and not to use it other than in connection with an evaluation of the Transaction, (iii) require that any Representative other than a director or officer of Disclosing Party or Receiving Party execute a counterpart of this Agreement prior to any disclosure by the Receiving Party of Confidential Information to such Representative, which counterpart shall have annexed thereto a schedule (the "Disclosure Schedule") setting forth in appropriate detail the Confidential Information that is to be disclosed to such Representative.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will (i) inform each of its Representatives receiving Confidential Information of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the existence and the terms of this Agreement, (ii) direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and not to use it other than in connection with an evaluation of the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for Representatives to execute a counterpart of the agreement and Disclosure Schedule, the contract lacks a mechanism for ensuring those Representatives are legally bound to confidentiality. While the Receiving Party is obligated to inform Representatives of the confidentiality, the omission eliminates a direct legal obligation for Representatives, creating an inconsistency. Section 4, where the Receiving Party is held responsible for inadvertent disclosure, is weakened as there's now no direct contractual obligation on the Representatives themselves.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text required representatives to execute a counterpart of the agreement, which directly enforced confidentiality. The changed text omits this requirement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, "The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure..." The original text ensured that representatives 'agree' via contract, while the changed text leaves the agreement implied, weakening the enforceability of the 'best efforts' clause and contradicting the Receiving Party's responsibility for Representative actions.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Affiliate" shall mean any Person that, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified. As used in the foregoing definition, the term "Person" shall mean an individual, firm, trust, association, corporation, partnership, government (whether federal, state, local or other political subdivision, or any agency or bureau of any of them) or other entity.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Affiliate" shall mean any Person that, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the definition of 'Person' within the definition of 'Affiliate' creates ambiguity. Section 8, regarding non-solicitation, refers to 'Affiliates'. With the 'Person' definition removed, it's unclear if non-solicitation extends to individuals, governments, or just corporate entities. This creates a contradiction because it restricts actions regarding 'Affiliates,' but doesn't clearly define the scope of who or what an affiliate includes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other or any of its Affiliates on the date of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Person' within the 'Affiliate' definition, which included individuals, firms, trusts, etc. The changed text removes this definition, leading to ambiguity in the contradicted text. The contradicted text restricts solicitation of employees of 'Affiliates'. Without the 'Person' definition, it's unclear if this restriction applies to individuals, government entities, or only corporate affiliates, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the scope of the non-solicitation agreement is now ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of six months following the date of this Agreement, each of the parties and its affiliates (as defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act), will not (and will not assist or encourage others to) directly or indirectly, without the written consent of the other party: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership (including but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Exchange Act) of any of the other party's assets or businesses or any securities issued by the other party, or any bank debt, claims or other obligations of the other party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, directly or from a third party;
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of six months following the date of this Agreement, each of the parties and its affiliates will not directly or indirectly, without the written consent of the other party: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership of any of the other party's assets or businesses or any securities issued by the other party, or any bank debt, claims or other obligations of the other party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, directly or from a third party;
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(as defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act)', we are removing the defintion of Affiliate within the Standstill Agreement, causing a contradiction because the document then has it undefined in this section. The term 'affiliates' is used in this section as the definition to stand by the agreement, but it doesn't have an existing definition causing the agreement to not be fully valid
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of six months following the date of this Agreement, each of the parties and its affiliates will not directly or indirectly, without the written consent of the other party: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership of any of the other party's assets or businesses or any securities issued by the other party, or any bank debt, claims or other obligations of the other party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, directly or from a third party;
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'affiliates' with reference to Rule 12b-2. The changed text removes this definition, creating ambiguity around the scope of the standstill agreement, potentially contradicting other sections (including the contradicted text that's a copy of the Changed Text) that rely on a clear definition of 'affiliates' to enforce the agreement, causing an in-text contradiction as outlined in the Omissions definition.
